turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: We'll resume arguments in Doran against Salem Inn. Mr. Kassner.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Thank you Mr. Chief Justice and may it please the Court. I think that initially I should review the facts which were scantly reviewed yesterday because there seems to be some misapprehension as to what actually took place. Appellees herein where owners and operators of premises in which food and liquor was served; one for a period of eight years, one for a period of five years and one for a period of about two years. At the time the town of North Hempstead passed its ordinance, during that period, they had offered their patrons topless dancing entertainment. The ordinance forbade the appearance in any public place and underlying any public place of a person with the breast uncovered or with the lower part of the torso uncovered. This was the broadest imaginable prohibition, it covered every place in the town of North Hempstead and it specifically covered any scene, sketch, act, or entertainment. In that regard, it was clearly affecting a theatrical performance presumptively protected by the First Amendment. Now, to -- [Voice Overlap] -- it wasn't covering anything but live people.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: The appellees did not offer entertainment by a way of topless waitresses. They did not offer topless bartenders. What they had was, a real stage, segregated from the patrons on which professional dancers were employed throughout the day to offer dancing entertainment. These professional dancers were hired through theatrical agencies. Some of them were employed in ballet work in the City of New York and did this to earn extra money. This wasn't a situation of -- which was alluded to in LaRue I believe to (Inaudible) in a place it served liquor. Now, the appellants herein maintain in page 12 their brief the method exercised by the legislative body to ensure the health, safety and well-being of the community is not a proper subject for review by this Court. I don't know whether they're serious or not serious in that contention but it would seem that the statute was enacted. The ordinance was enacted under this theory. It seems that they denied the existence of a supremacy clause. They claim that they could undercut the evils, various nuisance evils at the source by passing this type of an overbroad speech inhibiting ordinance.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Kassner, am I right in thinking that your claim is basically one of overbreadth that is that New York could have prohibited these particular performances in institutions serving liquor had it drawn an ordinance to that effect?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Had the state liquor authority in the State of New York drawn an ordinance as sanctioned under LaRue involving the licensee, the sale of liquor in premises where this type of activity went on, yes they could've done so Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Do you say the state liquor authority but you went into federal court to challenge this provision. We don't know whether New York, we know that New York has this power under the Twenty-first Amendment, we don't know whether it resides in the state liquor authority or whether it's delegated at the town of Hempstead. I think you pretty well have waived that by failing to litigate that in the state courts.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, Your Honor I haven't litigated that here, I don't think I need it. I'll stick with my overbreadth and I'm just mentioning as an aside that the town of North Hempstead wouldn't have had the power to legislate the type of LaRue ordinance which is permissible but that is not necessary for the purpose of --

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Why not?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Because --

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: It isn't regulating liquor is just saying there won't be any nude dancing in bars.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: That's not what they've said in this ordinance You Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: I know but let's assume they did. They had a local ordinance so there shall be no nude dancing in any place for liquors so is that what you say -- that's not within the power of municipality?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor. Not within the power of the town of North Hempstead.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: That's what you're saying?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: Is that an issue before us?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: No Your Honor. But that's -- if you asked that's what I'm saying, yes, that's what I'm saying but that's not issue before here Your Honor. They didn't say no topless dancing in the town of North Hempstead. They didn't say no topless dancing where liquor was sold in the town of North Hempstead. They said, No exposure of the breast or the lower part of the torso in the town of North Hempstead in any public place, whether it'd be part of a sketch, scene, act, or entertainment or otherwise. So, I'm willing to deal with what they did say rather than what they didn't say and I'm not depending upon the fact that they couldn't have passed the LaRue type statute. There is a contention by Mr. Darago made yesterday that the overbreadth adjudication is limited to the spoken word and all I need to say is that overbreadth adjudication is limited to expression protected by the First Amendment and or presumptively protected by the First Amendment or expression which could be protected by the First Amendment and that includes a great deal more than the spoken work. He claims that the ordinance was aimed at the elimination of litter, noise, traffic congestion and unspecified conduct of patrons adjacent to appellees premises by a cutting-off at the source. It should be noted that there are ample laws in the State of New York to cover those evils of litter, noise, traffic congestion, and whatever unspecified conduct of patrons they maybe referring to that if there weren't adequate laws in the State of New York or the town of North Hempstead, these evils could've been cope with by a more narrowly drawn specifically directed piece of legislation rather than what is referred to as a cutting off at the source. It would be just as appropriate for a town to forbid sporting events because of the traffic congestion incident to a stadium or to forbid political rallies because of the congestion incident to the area where it take place as it is to pass this type of ordinance to cope with traffic congestion or noise or litter. There is no doubt of what the intention of the town of North Hempstead was. What I am arguing here today under the doctrine of overbreadth is that the law which they passed was far too broad to be permitted under the First Amendment.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Are you going to get the request Mr. Kassner whether in any event this Court should've proceeded with the suit libel tendency before its judgment of criminal prosecution against one of these three bars?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: If you want me to move to that Mr. Justice Brennan, I'll move to that --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: That question is here.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor. I was going to --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: You're maybe right at everything but wouldn't be immaterial I think if both the District Court shouldn't have preceded with the suit.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: On the issue of abstention, we have here two plaintiffs.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Well, is this abstention, how do you --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well I'm using abstention in Younger versus Harris sense is that --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Is that abstention.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well Your Honor--

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: It's alright. I don't blame you, I don't know that any of us knows.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I've read you a decision in Steffel and I've read your decision in Dombrowski -- all I can say is that I am willing to assume that the Younger doctrine is classified as abstention despite the fact that the Pullman doctrine was classified as abstention and there are two different facets of an abstention issue if you don't want me to use the word abstention, I'll use comity --

turns.name: JUSTICE STEWART
turns.text: Let's use the Younger doctrine.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: The Younger doctrine, yes. As far as I can tell from my reading of Younger, the holding in Younger was that we're at the time of the filing of a federal suit, the federal plaintiff was then at that time a defendant and a pending criminal proceeding in a single pending criminal proceeding. The federal court would not take the case and send it back for adjudication in the state criminal proceeding.

turns.name: JUSTICE STEWART
turns.text: Now, it would dismiss.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor,It would dismiss.

turns.name: JUSTICE STEWART
turns.text: Just send it back.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: It would dismiss. Now we have two plaintiffs here who where never involved in a pending state criminal prosecution either before or after the commenced to the federal action. Now, as to them it appears clear that Younger does not apply. Now, I will get to this argument of privity later but under your decisions in Roe versus -- I think Wade, Roe versus Wade. You have the same idetical situation of one man who was involved in a state criminal prosecution and one man who wasn't it was dismissed as to the one who was. The Court took jurisdiction and the term to the question to the one who doesn't. So, I don't this is a noble issue in this case. I think that the only issue trully in this case substantive or procedurals is how do you treat M & L? I dont think there's any question as to the other two plaintiffs on the substantive or procedural issues. So if you want me to go to how do you treat M & L and why do you treat M & L that way, I will and attack the heart of what I believe is the only problem left in this case after your decision is obvious of production. Not M & L after the commencement of the federal case violated the statute and were subjected to immediate prosecution. I will get back to the fact that they were subjective to eight criminal prosecutions at the period of four or five days later and bring that back under the Younger doctrine but right now I'll assume that they had been subjected to a single criminal prosecution in the State Court after the commencement and the file of the federal action by the filing of a federal complaint.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Do I understand that the criminal prosecution followed by a day in the following in the filing of the federal complaint?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes, the first criminal prosecution followed a day by the filing of a federal complaint because they started violating the ordinance the day after the federal complaint was filed and the motion for the temporary restraining order was denied. That particular plaintiff, M & L, did not have the staying power of the other two. It was a question of economics, they were being destroyed and they couldn't accept this destruction of their business in the interim period that would take to determine either the federal or the state action. They therefore determined to go ahead and do it on the theory that what they were doing would be vindicated in the end. The question is who should vindicate what they were doing the State Court or the Federal Court. Now, Judge Oakes in his decision below wrote approximately a page and a half on this on the considerations of treating M & L differently or the same as the other two plaintiffs and frankly I can't improve on either his words or his reasoning and it would be foolish for me to try. He specified certain consideration which dictated that M & L should be treated the same as the other two plaintiffs. Now, with the other two plaintiffs, you had no problem, he just said that it is not Younger case and he pass on from that.

turns.name: JUSTICE STEWART
turns.text: Neither the other two plaintiffs ever was prosecuted in the state courts during the -- during any dependency of the federal --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Never Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE STEWART
turns.text: Litigation.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes, never.

turns.name: JUSTICE STEWART
turns.text: And that's because I gather they complied with your --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor. That is correct. They had the staying power to be able to do so.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: With M & L did -- have they still, what are they doing now?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: M & L is still doing topless dancing.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: And where is the state prosecution as of right now?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Waiting for this Court's determination.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: And so you -- you represent them in the state composite?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I do Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: So, you have the case going in the State Court and the Federal Court altogether.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, the state court has not proceeded at all. It's remaining right where it was the date that they were arrested.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: That's what you were driving forward to get state court among it.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, I wasn't driving to get the state, excuse me.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: Well, why did they violate the law?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: They violated the law --

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: The state got the move.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: They violated the law which they believed was unconstitutional because they believe they would be out of business before either court adjudicated the issue Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: So, well you do want it and one of the other courts?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, and if the issue was adjudicated in the Federal Court --

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: In one of the other or not?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: You wanted one of the other?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: Or both?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: No. One of the other preferably the Federal Court because that's quicker.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: Aren't you running right into Younger?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I don't believe I'm running into Younger.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: Is it about now?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: If M & L were the only --

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: If you have two court proceeding going at the same time --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well --

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: On the same point.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: The question becomes who initiated the second court proceeding? At the time of the filing of federal complaint, the local authorities will put on notice that there is a substantial federal question involved in the ordinance.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: But the temporary restraining order was denied.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: In the Federal Court?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor. The temporary restraining order was denied.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: The difference you have now have an injunction against the enforcement of that criminal proceeding?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Did you get that from the Court of Appeals?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I got that first from the District Court, it was upheld by the Court of Appeals.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: Got it from the second district judge that you went to, was that not? So you went to Judge Dooling first?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Judge Dooling was an emergency judge. The case was assigned to Judge Bartels.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: And it went to Bartels and Bartels entered the restraining order, did he not?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: It's after a hearing and after about 20 or 30 days after reading briefs and after a hearing, he entered a preliminary injunction.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: it's an injunction which has stop criminal proceeding, isn't it?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: The criminal proceeding for all intensive purposes was stopped as soon as it was initiated.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: You mean the state didn't proceed with it?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: The state did no proceed with it, there was no intention --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: But now they can't without violating any --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: No. They can't without violating the federal injunction.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: What was the terms of the injunction?

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: This was a preliminary injunction.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor. The terms of the injunction whether the state was in the -- defendants were enjoined from enforcing the ordinance.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Against anybody?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Against these three plaintiff.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Against anybody or not?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: As far as I know, against these three plaintiffs.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: Well didn't they hold it than --

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: As far as you know, having you got the injunction, where is the injunction. Is it in the appendix?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes it's in the jurisdictional state -- Page 23(a) of the jurisdictional statement. Just the plaintiffs --

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: But if the ordinance is primary declared was the District Court and affirmed by the Court of Appeals was declared unconstitutional on its face, was it not?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: The Court of Appeals said that there was a likelihood of success. They did not declare it unconstitutional as they couldn't because it was merely a preliminary injunction.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Well, what's at the top of page 23(a)?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, 23(a) is the District Court, I'm talking about the Circuit Court now and in Appendix 4(a), Judge Oakes' decision says initially, in answer to the question of the propriety of the injunctive relief granted here, we agreed with the District Court as to the probability of success on the merits.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Now, was that about 16 to 17 lines history for the citation to burn, what about that sentence?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: As such the ordinance would have to fall that describes the ordinance as if as such that ordinance would have to fall. Your Honor, I think he meant to make this final determination in their preliminary injunction motion.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Well, certainly the District Court at the top of page 23(a) says in conclusion, we find that Local Law No. 1-1973, the town of Hempstead is on its phase violative of plaintiff's First Amendment Rights, that's no suggestion that they may, will very well succeed, that's a flat statement.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: That is correct. But I don't think you can attribute that to Judge Oakes' decision. That is clear but Judge Oakes' decision is not that clear, he seems to say probability of success and use those words in the proper context for the proper purpose.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Furthermore, are you suggesting that the affirmants do not include what Mr. Justice Rehnquist just read to you, is that it?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: But his--but what Judge Oakes says is we agree with the District Court as to the probability of success on the merits.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: It construed the District --

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: What it construed, the District Court's opinion as being one of probability of success, that's what it amounts to which is zero.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: I think -- you can -- the District Court of Appeals maybe narrow in planning and (Inaudible) --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: No Your Honor. I don't think anybody is damage.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Then what is your right on the probability of -- on eight probabilities there.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor, I don't --

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: I'll follow the --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I don't think anybody is damage by that.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Well the state is pretty (Inaudible) there, if he'd understood that if it did not enforced this ordinance against anybody because of the declaration of unconstitutionality with that as precisely what Mayo against Herington in this court was designed to do that.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: But I don't -- those aren't the words he used in his declaration on page 23(a).

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: But you declared unconstitutional on its face. Well didn't enter an injunction against every decision.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: That is correct.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: But more important what you declared unconstitutional on its face and most of the prosecutors until it's finally settled which I will work on of course you are against anybody. They aren't forcing it against anybody in this -- I don't know --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, I think at that time, these were the only three that were doing the activity in the town.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Well they --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: The activity of topless dancing. So, they had all the parties before them in my impression. These role -- the people could've been covered by these all three came into Court together, there was no class action because you had everybody to start out with.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: What more would -- what more remained to be done before Judge Bartels after his order of September 6?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I think something was done -- well, no, an appeal was taken.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: But I mean, he says therefore pending the final determination of this action but what more remained to be done after he had found the ordinance unconstitutional on its phase.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I guess they would have to file an answer and I would have to make a motion for summary judgment.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: But that was all pro forma after this determination, wasn't it?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, if you take his words to mean that this was not to be found a probability of success sufficient to warrant an injunction, if you assume that that's not what he meant when he said, what he said, then he would undoubtedly follow his prior determination on the motion for summary judgment but certainly did end the case, you had to do something. There would have to be motion for summary judgment after he joined the revision, there would have to be a permanent injunction entered and there could've been new facts brought out.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: What if nobody did anything after the order of September 6? Then it simply remains in status quo idea.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, somebody had to file an answer to -- somebody had to make a motion for summary judgment in order to bring it to an end if there wouldn't have been an appeal for the Circuit Court on the preliminary injunction.

turns.name: JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Well, but if nothing is done, Judge Bartels' temporary injunction remains in effect these town as enjoined and it can go on that way I take for 20 years.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes, If nothing is done, that would be effect of any preliminary injunction. It wouldn't be unusual.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: I gather your suggestion Mr. Kassner, Judge Oakes at least was conscious of the problem raised by Mayo was he?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: He did side it.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: He didn't side it but Judge Oaks a good judge and he knew what he was doing when he wrote probability of success.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: If he did, why did he write as is to the Court in which it would have to fall? That's a little inconsistent, isn't it?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Sometimes, you --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Well, at least the issue would be clearer if he had not used that sentence, would it not?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes, it would, but I don't think that that should be made to overwrite the words probability of success which are absolutely clear.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Probably, (Inaudible) said, the District Court said that he probably use the sense at zero. (Inaudible)

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, if you want to --

turns.name: JUSTICE STEWART
turns.text: Probability of success is 100%.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, if you want to construe Judge Oakes meaning that, I don't construe his meaning that because he wasn't asked called unto rule on that and he was called unto rule on probability of success, he used the words probability of success, he meant that the statute was overbroad in his mind at that point, there was a probability of success because of the apparent overbreadth of the statute and by not sticking in the word apparent or I don't know whether you can make his opinion be a final determination or motion for preliminary injunction.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: Well any of that Mr. Kassner, you going to get neither the question whether because you got into the federal court first that takes care of the Younger problem and even in the M & L or whether would it --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: M & L.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: M & L?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes. All as I said before, on this point, all I'm going to do is merely restate what Judge Oakes said because I can improve on it. He spoke about equity principles granting relief to two people in the same shoes as a third person with respect towards --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: That's your case but suppose we didn't have the other two parties, all we have was M & L?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I'd be in trouble. Yes I would. Because, I don't think I should be but I know that I would be --

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: You don't think that --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I don't think I should be, no.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: If you won the race to the courthouse door by getting in the Federal Court --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I think that should determine it because I think that that's the only reasonable fixed way to determine jurisdiction. The state courts are no more entitled to adjudicate First Amendment Rights in the Federal Court. I could make an excellent argument for the fact that the federal court is the prime court for the adjudication of constitutional rights.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Well it could (Inaudible)

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I don't know whether I'd lose that argument.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: No and Younger is an exception to that. Younger is an exception to that because of doctrines of committee but there is no committee involved to remove Federal Court jurisdiction what's the Younger problem itself does not arise and the Younger problem is a pending criminal prosecution of the federal plaintiff at the time of the filing of a federal complaint. That's what Younger says. Now, I was disturbed by Younger but Younger is the law and that is the law.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: And look at --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I'm willing to live with Younger but I don't want to see Younger expanded to --

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: And you could be Younger, you won the race to the courthouse.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Excuse me Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: Looking at Younger, you won the race --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes I did, yes I did.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: You first told your clients you better bide by this ordinance.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I did.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: Then you ran into the Federal Court.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: And continued to tell them --

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: Wait a minute, and then you said no, we got to get in the State Court too so you go violate the law now where you give them State Court. And that had been an added multi court around you to go in that so.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: No Judge Marshall, you know I didn't do that --

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: Then go to North Hempstead.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: You know I didn't do that. Why did I want to create a Younger type problem?

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: But you didn't go to the Admiralty Court, I agree.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I advise all three of my clients to obey the ordinance at least pending the preliminary injunction determination. Two of my clients followed my instructions; the third client said he couldn't take it.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: (Inaudible)

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, the denial of a TRO is not in adjudication of anything under merits.

turns.name: JUSTICE MARSHALL
turns.text: They're in very good shape.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: I know that -- that al three would've violated the ordinance as it was written but I didn't and I told them not to violate the ordinance and two obeyed and one didn't and the one didn't because of economic exigencies. He couldn't stay in business with the cut on business and that's why he had to go about it. Now, as long as we're talking Younger, why don't we talk about eight prosecutions in a period of three or four days? Now, Younger clearly held, there is no suggestion that this single prosecution against Harris is brought in bad faith or as only one of a series of repeated prosecutions to which you will be subjected. I don't think Younger applies if he would've been subjected in a prosecution before he filed his federal complaint. I don't think the Younger case anyway.

turns.name: JUSTICE BRENNAN
turns.text: That's a harassment exception.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Yes Your Honor. I mean, Younger is clear on that point. They serve three summonses, then they had five arrests and they closed the premises on four separate occasions

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Do you thinks that's -- do you think that's true even though a TRO is denied and even though that the substantial issue about the preliminary injunction.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well, the --

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: What's the state suppose to do, (Inaudible) rush to the federal court for enforcing its federal laws?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: They had this going on for eight years before. They could've waited 10 or 20 days.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: My question is what do you suppose -- the state prosecutor supposed to do?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Stay its hand until the judge determines the issue in the federal court.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: (Inaudible) he files federal complaint, he supposed to put enforce and laws, is that it?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: It depends on what law you're talking about Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: We're talking about this one.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: Well if I were the -- if I were the local prosecutor and I saw that there was a substantial federal question involved in the ordinance, I might wait the 10 or 15 days that it would take the federal district judge to adjudicate the issue and not quite create the abrasion that they themselves are complaining about.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: Well, I understand you -- I am understanding your point, what about your point. We'll just give way to --

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: My client should've waited.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: And what adjudicate it -- what would he had done at the preliminary injunction has been denied.

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: He would've been in more trouble than he was with the granting of the preliminary injunction and what would he have done? He might have continued, he might have stopped.

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: What was the prosecutor suppose to do?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: The prosecutor under the --

turns.name: JUSTICE WHITE
turns.text: The final adjudication?

turns.name: HERBERT KASSNER
turns.text: No, Your Honor. After a determination of the preliminary injunction hearing -- on hearings and on briefs, if it was determined that that should not be granted then I don't think the prosecutor is bound to wait any additional time but I think that he could've waited the 10 or 15 days that the rule have taken to avoid this abrasion that he complains about himself but I don't justify my client' conduct.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE BURGER
turns.text: Thank you Mr. Kassner. Thank you gentlemen. The case is submitted.
